text: ‘Did not see, in the rank of Godhead, a prize to be coveted’; others would render, ‘thought it no usurpation to claim the rank of Godhead’.
‘Dwelling in the glory’; the Greek is perhaps more naturally rendered ‘to the glory’.
‘Who shares my thoughts as he does’; the Greek would also bear the meaning, ‘who has a heart like his’.

‘You must know’; the Latin represents this as if the Apostle were informing the Philippians of something they did not know, but the Greek may equally well imply that it was something they knew already.
